recent_trades.summary: Initiating Intel on dip with strong 30-day momentum and positive sentiment, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC shows a 2.44% 30-day gain and high positive sentiment (+0.46), with the stock pulling back from its 30-day high. A 2% starter position capitalizes on the semiconductor cycle and news-driven optimism.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing Oracle on negative sentiment and recent overbought conditions, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has rallied +9.17% but faces negative sentiment (-0.07) and a potential pullback after a strong 30-day run. Trimming to 2% captures gains and reallocates capital.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Eli Lilly after an all-time high to lock in profits, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY has surged +8.11% since purchase, reaching an all-time high. Positive news flow is priced in, and trimming to 2.5% reduces risk while maintaining exposure to its strong drug pipeline.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting CAT — weakest holding at -1% unrealized loss, declining from 30-day highs with neutral sentiment and fading momentum into close.
recent_trades.reasoning: CAT dropped from 905 highs to 888.66, down -0.2% today and -0.95% over 5 days. It's the only holding with a negative unrealized P&L. Neutral sentiment offers no catalyst for reversal. Freeing up ~$3,350 in cash for better opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 4% CSCO position on upgraded PT, cybersecurity tailwinds and +0.43 sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: CSCO has strong positive coverage including BofA raising its price target post-Q3 and recommendations to buy cybersecurity leaders; chart is stable with slight gains and not currently held in portfolio. New 4% position fits diversification goals near close with high conviction on the fundamental news.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AVGO to 4% target due to negative sentiment on AI supply constraints despite today's gain.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO sentiment is -0.18 with headlines highlighting Nvidia's 20X supply demand and mixed AI bottleneck coverage; although +1.3% intraday the longer-term chart shows resistance near 430. Reducing from 6% to free capital and manage risk in a name not recently traded.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing MSFT to 10% target on positive sentiment, active S&P mentions, and stable chart near highs.
recent_trades.reasoning: MSFT carries +0.49 sentiment with repeated ChartMill notes on active Dow/S&P stocks; 30D performance is modestly positive with low volatility. Adding from current ~8% weight as a defensive core tech holding with room to 10% without breaching concentration limits.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the tiny CAT stub because the position is immaterial and the chart is weaker than our other industrial holdings, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CAT is a very small 1.3% position, down over the last 5 days, and trading below its recent highs while GE and DE show stronger momentum. Closing the stub simplifies the portfolio and frees a bit of capital for higher-conviction leaders.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM slightly because it remains below its recent highs while other semiconductor holdings show cleaner momentum, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM has neutral sentiment, is down on a 5-day basis, and remains meaningfully below its 30-day high despite the broader semiconductor bid. Since NVDA, AMD, and now ORCL offer stronger trend confirmation, reducing QCOM from about 3% to 2% improves relative-strength quality without fully exiting.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a new position in Duke Energy at the 30-day low with very high positive sentiment, targeting 2% weight, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: DUK sentiment is the highest in the universe at +0.74, yet the stock is near its 30-day low, suggesting a potential sentiment-price divergence. Adding a 2% defensive position capitalizes on the bullish news flow and hurricane preparedness grants.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Amazon on strong 5-day momentum, positive logistics news, and favorable sentiment, targeting 3.5% weight, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is up 1.37% over 5 days, sentiment is +0.43, and a new $10B USPS–DHL last-mile deal could benefit its logistics network. Increasing from 2.07% to 3.5% captures momentum with room below the 20% cap.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Exxon Mobil to 1.5% weight to reduce energy exposure amid volatile oil geopolitics, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: XOM has a 3.6% unrealized loss and oil markets face uncertainty with Iran ceasefire talks and airstrikes. Reducing from 2.95% to 1.5% lowers portfolio risk while retaining some energy exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Selling EOG Resources as energy sentiment remains mixed and the position is a drag, rotating into growth names, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: EOG is down 1.12% in a volatile energy environment with conflicting signals from Iran ceasefire hopes and U.S. airstrikes. The 2.5% position lacks a near-term catalyst, making it a candidate for reallocation.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Air Products entirely due to underperformance and no positive catalysts, freeing cash for stronger opportunities, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: APD is down 2.1% with a 30-day decline, has no news catalyst, and the materials sector is not showing relative strength. Reallocating the 1.97% position to higher-conviction names.

recent_trades.summary: Re-entering NVDA at 4% — Jensen Huang's bold market cap prediction and positive sentiment shift warrant a small position after selling earlier, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Sold NVDA earlier at a loss but the stock has bounced +0.6% today with positive sentiment (+0.50). Headlines about Nvidia demanding 20x more supply and Jensen's market cap prediction are bullish. The 30-day trend is +0.57%. Taking a modest 4% position to participate in potential continuation.

recent_trades.summary: Adding a bit to AMZN as it presses the top of its 30-day range with steady positive sentiment, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is trading essentially at its 30-day high with constructive intraday action and positive consumer/logistics-related news flow. This is a smaller add because we bought recently and there is no major new company-specific catalyst, but the technical position remains favorable into the close.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY as fresh obesity and diabetes headlines reinforce momentum near all-time highs, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY has the clearest fundamental catalyst set in this universe today, with new ADA data presentations and coverage expansion headlines supporting the existing uptrend. Even after a recent buy, the stock remains near the top of its 30-day range and the news is genuinely incremental, so a moderate increase still fits the setup.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming RTX slightly as it fades below recent highs while stronger industrial names in the portfolio continue to lead, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: RTX is positive on the month but has stalled below its 30-day high and intraday action looks softer than GE and DE. News flow is mildly supportive, not strong enough to justify keeping it as one of the larger industrial weights, so I would reduce it modestly to free capital for higher-momentum holdings.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming META to reduce concentration as the largest holding after a -1.2% move today.
recent_trades.reasoning: META at 9.97% weight with slight underperformance despite +0.19 sentiment and subscription-growth headlines; near-term technicals show mild reversal. Reducing to 8.0% frees capital for higher-momentum names while respecting the 20% cap and avoiding full exit.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to QCOM after solid 3.5% intraday gain and positive AI chip coverage.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM up 3.5% today with +0.17 sentiment, news linking it to AI design stocks and sustainable dividends; chart shows breakout above 20-period SMA. Lifting from 6.3% to 8.0% as it fits AI theme without exceeding position limits and uses available liquidity.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing NVDA on strong positive sentiment and Jensen Huang's bold new market-cap prediction.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA sentiment +0.50 with headlines highlighting AI leadership and $1T+ sales expectations; technicals hold near 30D highs with low volatility. Raising from 4.0% to 6.0% uses part of cash balance as conviction remains high in continued AI infrastructure demand.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 3% position in Thermo Fisher on strong 5-day and 30-day performance and positive sentiment, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: TMO is up 1.87% over 5 days and 3.02% over 30 days, breaking out with volume. The company unveiled new innovations at ASMS 2026, and life sciences tools are a growth area. This adds quality healthcare exposure replacing UNH.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting remaining Intuitive Surgical position after prior trim; weak price action persists, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ISRG is down 2.56% from our cost and has shown no signs of recovery. We earlier trimmed to 1.5%, and today's data provides no reason to hold. Selling fully eliminates a drag on performance.

recent_trades.summary: Selling Mastercard to consolidate payment positions; underperformance vs. peers warrants exit, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA has lagged, down 0.85% unrealized, with no positive catalyst in today's news. We hold Visa and American Express, so trimming MA reduces overlap and frees cash for higher-conviction names.

recent_trades.summary: Selling UnitedHealth as it hits 30-day low with negative momentum and no near-term catalysts, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH is down 1.4% over 30 days and 0.93% over 5 days, trading at the low of its range. Sentiment is neutral, and healthcare insurance faces headwinds. We rotate out to improve portfolio momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to FCX on strong +4.1% session move with stock near 30-day highs, copper demand tailwinds, and materials sector strength today.
recent_trades.reasoning: FCX is up +4.1% today and +4.14% over 30 days, trading near its 30-day high of $65.52 vs current $65.46. The current position is only 4.52%, leaving room to add to 7%. Copper sector is showing broad strength (CPER also presumably positive). No negative news overhang. Increasing from ~77 shares to approximately 120 shares target.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting UNH as it continues its downtrend (-1.4% over 30 days, -0.93% over 5 days) with neutral sentiment and sitting at its 30-day low.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH is at $381.35 which matches its 30-day low exactly, indicating ongoing selling pressure. The 30-day performance is -1.40% and 5-day is -0.93%, showing consistent deterioration. Sentiment is neutral at -0.04. No positive catalysts are visible in the news. Freeing up ~6% of portfolio weight to redeploy into higher-momentum names or hold as cash buffer.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting DIS — stock drifting near 30-day lows with minimal upside catalysts despite mildly positive sentiment, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS at $103.57 is near its 30-day low of $103.10, down -0.2% today. Position is small at 3% with -0.75% unrealized loss. The '3 reasons to buy' headline is generic. Capital better deployed elsewhere. Frees ~$3,400.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting remaining UNH position — stock at 30-day low of $381.35 with persistent weakness and no positive catalysts, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: UNH has been in steady decline, now at the exact 30-day low. Already trimmed from 6% to 3% earlier today. Neutral sentiment, no positive catalysts in headlines. Freeing up ~$3,400 in cash for better opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4.5% position in Nvidia to capture ongoing AI momentum fueled by management's confident growth forecasts.
recent_trades.reasoning: Nvidia continues to dominate the AI infrastructure space, and CEO Jensen Huang's recent bullish market cap predictions reinforce conviction. We are deploying capital freed up from our financial exits to capture Nvidia's unparalleled pricing power and demand visibility.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting our remaining Goldman Sachs position as management's warnings on inflation risks reduce our near-term conviction.
recent_trades.reasoning: Goldman Sachs' COO recently called inflation the single biggest risk to the economy. We are fully exiting our 4% position to reallocate capital into secular growth themes, specifically AI hardware, which are less sensitive to macroeconomic cyclicality.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting NEE entirely after mild underperformance and lack of positive catalysts today, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEE is down 0.5% with the weakest technical picture among utilities and no supportive headlines in this run. Realizing the small 3.73% position frees capital for higher-conviction tech/healthcare ideas. Avoids repeating recent trades on other names.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AMD to 5% target on its 4.5% outperformance and AI chip momentum, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD shows the strongest intraday gain in the universe with bullish technicals breaking above recent highs; news flow around Nvidia's targets indirectly supports the sector. Position was only 1.05% so this brings it closer to peer weights without exceeding 20% cap. No recent trade on AMD so this is not redundant.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 2% position in PepsiCo at the 30-day low on positive sentiment and defensive value, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: PEP is trading at its 30-day low of $145.77 with a sentiment score of +0.33. It offers a stable consumer staple hedge against inflation and market uncertainty. The stock typically rebounds from these levels, and the yield adds a margin of safety. We prefer this over other beaten-down names due to brand strength and consistent cash flows.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Oracle slightly after an 8.6% gain to fund a new defensive position, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has performed well and sits at 3.12% of the portfolio. Reducing it slightly to ~2.8% frees up cash while still maintaining significant exposure to the software sector. The move is purely a rebalancing to fund PEP, not a loss of conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Apple to lock in a 10% gain and reduce exposure, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AAPL has rallied over 10% from our cost basis and now represents a 1.49% weight. While the long-term story remains positive, taking partial profits allows us to reallocate into a beaten-down name with higher near-term upside. We are reducing the position to ~0.75%.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting XOM as oil faces headwinds from a potential US-Iran ceasefire extension and inflation concerns, with the position marginally underwater, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: XOM is the only losing position (-0.16%) and faces macro headwinds: a US-Iran ceasefire extension would reduce geopolitical risk premium in oil prices. Reuters notes US inflation firming which could pressure demand. USO is down -2.1% today and -2.15% over 30 days. Despite a Mizuho target increase, the near-term oil outlook is uncertain. Freeing up this ~2.88% capital allocation for higher-conviction names like LLY and CSCO is preferable.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to CSCO on BofA price target raise following Q3 results and positive security revenue growth outlook, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CSCO sentiment is +0.43 (positive) with a concrete BofA price target raise post-Q3 earnings as the catalyst. Security revenue growth is cited as accelerating - a durable growth driver. Current weight is only 2.0% which is underweight given the positive setup. Increasing to 4% provides meaningful exposure while remaining modest. Price is near 30-day lows which offers an attractive entry point.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY as it hits an all-time high driven by CVS Zepbound coverage expansion and multiple upcoming ADA Scientific Sessions data presentations, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY sentiment is +0.37 (positive) with multiple concrete catalysts: CVS coverage boost for Zepbound directly expands addressable market, and upcoming ADA data presentations for Foundayo, Mounjaro, and retatrutide could serve as major catalysts. Price is hitting all-time highs with +9.83% unrealized gain already. Current weight is 9.34%; increasing to 12% capitalizes on genuine fundamental momentum with new news drivers not previously acted upon.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4% position in Cisco Systems on the back of a BofA price target upgrade and accelerating security revenue.
recent_trades.reasoning: Cisco is seeing positive momentum with a BofA price target upgrade and strong prospects for its security segment revenue growth. Establishing a 4% position captures this fundamental improvement while maintaining a diversified tech allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Goldman Sachs to 4% following management's cautionary comments on inflation risks to free up capital.
recent_trades.reasoning: Goldman Sachs' COO highlighted inflation as the single biggest risk to the economy, presenting a potential headwind for financials. Reducing the position from 8% to 4% locks in partial gains while freeing up cash.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating CAT at 4% target on AI-trade momentum and 59% rally mentioned in headlines, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CAT +0.8% today with neutral-to-positive news linking it to AI infrastructure; industrials like GE/HON/RTX already in portfolio but CAT offers diversification with 'buy point' technical setup. Small size fits within risk limits and uses trimmed capital from TJX.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY to reach 7% target on strong clinical data releases for Mounjaro/retatrutide and Zepbound coverage expansion, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY sentiment +0.37 with multiple fresh headlines on ADA presentations, new shingles vaccine tech, and ATH driven by obesity pipeline; this appears underpriced relative to peers as technicals consolidate near highs. Position sizing keeps it below 20% cap while using proceeds from TJX trim.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming TJX to 4% target after -1% underperformance and neutral sentiment, freeing capital for higher-conviction names.
recent_trades.reasoning: TJX chart shows recent weakness within its 30-day range; news is generic ('trending stock') with no fresh positive catalyst, unlike LLY's clinical updates or CAT's AI momentum. This trims an overweight position without exiting entirely.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to LLY as fresh obesity and diabetes data headlines support a breakout near all-time highs, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY has strong positive news flow today, including new ADA data and coverage expansion commentary, and the stock is firming after a shallow pullback with price back near the top of its 30-day range. The current weight is moderate, so increasing to 6% adds exposure to a defensive growth leader without creating concentration risk.

recent_trades.summary: Closing the last COP position as weak relative strength in energy still outweighs the supportive headlines, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: COP remains near its 30-day low with negative medium-term returns and unimpressive intraday recovery, which keeps it among the weakest charts in the book. The positive sentiment has not translated into price leadership, and removing the last small position creates capital for higher-quality momentum elsewhere.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining XOM stub because energy continues to lag and the position is too small to justify holding, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: XOM is sitting at the low end of its 30-day range with negative 5-day and 30-day performance, and the intraday chart still looks weaker than tech and industrial leaders. While news sentiment is mildly positive, oil-sensitive names remain under pressure amid ceasefire headlines, so freeing this small residual weight is the cleaner portfolio choice.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 2% position in SLV to add silver as an inflation hedge and electrification play, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Silver is up 3.1% in 30 days and benefits from firming inflation (per Reuters) and long-term electrification trends. We have no direct commodity exposure, and SLV provides a liquid, diversified way to capture silver's momentum without single-stock risk. The trade is funded by the ISRG trim and existing cash.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Intuitive Surgical to 1.5% due to weak price action and no near-term catalysts, freeing cash for a better opportunity.
recent_trades.reasoning: ISRG has declined 2.63% in our portfolio and lacks positive news, while healthcare exposure remains adequate through other holdings. Reducing its weight from 2.5% to 1.5% raises ~1% of portfolio value to partially fund a new commodity position.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming PM to free capital for LLY; PM faces regulatory headwinds and is down -1.18% over 30 days with modest unrealized gain.
recent_trades.reasoning: PM has declined -1.18% over 30 days and is -1.2% today. While sentiment is slightly positive (+0.11), the IQOS regulation questions noted in headlines create uncertainty. The position has only +2.22% unrealized gain. Trimming from 6.81% to ~4% frees capital for higher-conviction LLY position on new CVS catalyst.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

mvp_trade.summary: Trimming UNH from 6% to 3% — stock drifting to 30-day lows at $383.65 with neutral-to-negative sentiment and no positive catalyst, confidence 6/10.

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.58%, Nasdaq +0.91%, Dow +0.05%. Technology led sectors (+1.85%) while Consumer Staples lagged (-1.00%). GPT-5.4 led the day at +1.14%. 6 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek V4 Pro at +22.79%. Key headline: Exclusive | Amazon Strikes $6 Billion Deal With Snowflake for Its Agentic Computing Chips - WSJ — market reaction muted.
